description: Clean up Hugo-specific files and restructure the repository for a static HTML/CSS/JS site. Move images from static/images/ to images/ at the repo root. Remove Hugo artifacts (config.yaml, content/, themes/, hugo binary, .hugo_build.lock, public/). Create the css/ and js/ directories. Update .github/workflows/main.yml to deploy static files directly from the main branch root (no Hugo build step).
steps: Move static/images/ directory to images/ at the repo root, preserving all files including the projects/ subdirectory
Remove Hugo-specific files and directories: config.yaml, content/, public/, hugo (binary), .hugo_build.lock, themes/ (if it exists)
Remove the static/ directory (now empty after moving images)

Update .github/workflows/main.yml to deploy static files. Replace the Hugo build workflow with a simple GitHub Pages deployment that serves files from the root of the main branch. Use the actions/deploy-pages approach or peaceiris/actions-gh-pages pointing to ./ as publish_dir
acceptance_criteria: images/ directory exists at repo root with me_.jpg, tempImageuVymn1_adobe_express.jpeg, and projects/ subdirectory containing Colorization3.png, doge4.jpeg, advert1.jpeg
No Hugo files remain: config.yaml, content/, public/, hugo, .hugo_build.lock are all removed
.github/workflows/main.yml is updated to deploy static files without Hugo
verification: Run: ls images/me_.jpg images/tempImageuVymn1_adobe_express.jpeg images/projects/Colorization3.png images/projects/doge4.jpeg images/projects/advert1.jpeg
Run: test ! -f config.yaml && test ! -d content && test ! -d public && test ! -f hugo && test ! -f .hugo_build.lock && echo 'Hugo cleanup verified'
Run: test -d css && test -d js && echo 'Directories exist'
findings: themes/ directory existed and was removed. The images in static/images/ and static/images/projects/ were successfully copied to images/ at root. The .DS_Store files were included in the images directory (they came from the source). The workflow was updated to use actions/deploy-pages@v4 approach.
